canto_title: The Eclipse of Beatrice and the Celestial Motion

scenes.summary: The heavens erupt in a joyful hymn to the Holy Trinity, filling Dante with divine ecstasy.
One of the four faces begins to change color, warning Dante that the corruption of the Papacy has darkened the heavens.
The sky becomes covered in a dark cloud, mirroring a change in Beatrice's expression.
The transformed figure denounces the corruption of the Church, specifically the sale of sacred offices for profit.
The speaker laments the predatory nature of corrupt leaders and urges Dante to speak the truth.
A visual metaphor involving celestial vapor describes the shifting atmosphere of the heavens.
Beatrice directs Dante's gaze, revealing the movement of the Earth and the paths of ancient legends like Ulysses and Europa.
Dante's intense focus returns to Beatrice's radiant face, which outshines all other earthly or painted beauties.
A divine virtue physically propels Dante through the heavens with incredible speed.
Beatrice explains the divine design and the central role of the Divine Mind in the movement of the spheres.
Beatrice describes the mathematical and rhythmic relationship between the various celestial spheres.
Beatrice condemns how avarice drowns human will and erodes innate innocence and faith.
A metaphor of biological and behavioral decay illustrates how corruption alters the natural order.
Beatrice foretells a coming political and celestial realignment that will restore order and bring true fruit.
